config.section.virtual_computers.label: Virtual Computers
config.section.virtual_computers.desc: Disposable microVM computers with terminal, desktop, previews and agent access.
config.virtual_computers.disabled_desc: Enable the integration to launch and proxy boring-computers machines.
config.virtual_computers.private_note: boringd stays private. AuraGo proxies UI, previews and live channels through authenticated routes.
config.virtual_computers.control_plane_d: Connect AuraGo to the private boringd API and choose local installation or an external SSH host.
config.virtual_computers.tailscale_note: For Tailscale access, expose AuraGo with tsnet serve_http. Do not expose boringd directly.
help.virtual_computers.enabled: Enables the Virtual Computers UI and API routes.
help.virtual_computers.readonly: Allows status, listing, screenshots and downloads while blocking machine mutations.
help.virtual_computers.tool_enabled: Makes the virtual_computers native tool available to the agent.
help.virtual_computers.auto_setup: Allows AuraGo to run idempotent setup and repair steps on the selected local or SSH host.
help.virtual_computers.mode: Choose an external Ubuntu/KVM host over SSH or the local Ubuntu/Linux KVM host.
help.virtual_computers.host: Hostname or IP address of the external Ubuntu/KVM host. Ignored in local mode.
help.virtual_computers.ssh_port: SSH port for setup and repair.
help.virtual_computers.credential_id: AuraGo credential used for SSH setup. Secrets stay in the vault.
help.virtual_computers.install_dir: Directory where boring-computers is installed on the selected host.
help.virtual_computers.boringd_url: Private boringd URL from AuraGo's point of view, usually http://127.0.0.1:18082.
help.virtual_computers.default_template: Template used when launching without an explicit template.
help.virtual_computers.default_ttl_secon: Default machine lifetime. boringd supports 15 to 900 seconds.
help.virtual_computers.max_ttl_seconds: Maximum TTL AuraGo will request; capped to 900 seconds.
help.virtual_computers.max_running_machi: Policy limit shown to UI and tools for concurrent machines.
help.virtual_computers.allow_internet: Allows launches that request internet access.
help.virtual_computers.allow_persistent: Allows persistent machines. Keep off unless the host is trusted.
help.virtual_computers.allow_publish: Allows publish/save operations exposed by boringd.
help.virtual_computers.allow_agent_tasks: Allows boringd agent and shell-agent task channels.
help.virtual_computers.boring_token: Leave empty when AuraGo installs boringd; AuraGo generates this token and stores it in the Vault. Only enter a value when connecting to an existing boringd; it must match that host's BORING_TOKEN.
config.virtual_computers.local_host_note: Local installation requires Ubuntu/Linux with /dev/kvm, systemd, and root or passwordless sudo. boringd stays on 127.0.0.1:18082.
config.virtual_computers.sudo_password_s: A sudo password is securely stored in the Vault.
config.virtual_computers.sudo_password_m: No sudo password is stored. It is only needed when passwordless sudo is unavailable.
config.virtual_computers.sudo_password_s: Sudo password saved in the Vault.
config.virtual_computers.sudo_password_c: Sudo password removed from the Vault.
Do you really want to remove the centrally stored sudo password?
config.virtual_computers.sudo_password_s: Could not update the sudo password.
help.virtual_computers.sudo_password: AuraGo reuses the central sudo password from the Vault for local installation and repair. The same password is shared with execute_sudo and package management. Leave this empty when one is already stored or passwordless sudo works.
config.virtual_computers.storage_desc: S3-compatible storage for persistent boringd volumes.
help.virtual_computers.storage_endpoint: Host and port without a scheme, for example minio.local:9000.
help.virtual_computers.storage_bucket: Existing bucket used for boringd volumes.
help.virtual_computers.storage_region: Optional S3 region; blank uses us-east-1.
help.virtual_computers.storage_ssl: Uses HTTPS for the S3 connection.
help.virtual_computers.s3_access_key: Stores the access key only in the Vault.
help.virtual_computers.s3_secret_key: Stores the secret key only in the Vault.
